headline: The player corpus is 79.6% intermediate — a coaching goldmine hiding in plain sight
insight: Of 750 players detected, 79.6% are intermediate. However: skill estimation has only 45% reproducibility across re-analysis runs. Treat as directional, not precise.

pricing.reasoning: Hudl charges $200-2000/month for coaching analytics. At 1/10th for niche sport early stage: $80-$200/month for individual coaches, $500-$1000/month for coaching platforms.
pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
counter_argument: Skill estimates have 45% reproducibility (per verification). The 'intermediate' dominance may reflect prompt bias — Gemini may default to 'intermediate' when uncertain.

headline: Kitchen mastery is the #1 predictor of skill level — data aligns with coaching consensus
insight: Kitchen mastery by skill: {'advanced intermediate': 7.33, 'intermediate': 6.69, 'advanced_intermediate': 6.5, 'advanced': 7.5}. 'advanced' players avg 7.5/10. Note: these ratings are AI-estimated from video, not ground-truth measurements.

pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
counter_argument: Kitchen mastery being correlated with skill is already known by every coach. The novel claim is that AI can detect it from video — which has only 45% reproducibility.

headline: Average rally is 16.6 shots — 65.8% go epic (12+)
insight: From 187 clips: avg 16.6 shots/rally. 65.8% are 12+ shots. These are highlight clips (non-random), so actual play likely has shorter average rallies.
evidence: Rally stats: min=1, avg=16.6, max=45, n=187

pricing.note: Pickleball is ~1/100th the market of major sports. Pricing scaled accordingly.

sample_caveat: Based on 191 highlight clips from Courtana's CDN. Sample is non-random (auto-selected highlights, not sequential recording). Venue diversity unknown (97% unidentifiable, likely single-venue dominated). Verification test showed: brand detection 20% reproducible, shot counts 40%, skill estimates 45%, DUPR predictions 0%.
counter_argument: Highlight clips are selected for interesting play — rally lengths in this sample likely skew longer than population average. Need sequential (non-highlight) clips to validate.
